notable_excerpts: Given climate change, etc., you don't know how things pan out, number one. So, we remain flexible, agile, able to move in either direction.
Our ratings underscore our commitment to responsible business. We have upped the game on MSCI ratings, upped our score on S&P Global and done decently on all the other ratings.
Our innovation pipeline remains strong, with 15 new product launches throughout the year.
Tata Sampann had a strong quarter, 45% growth, all volume driven.
We opened 12 new stores during the quarter. We are now at 504.
Our consolidated revenue crossed the milestone of Rs. 5,000 crores this quarter, growing at 15%.
We have used AI to align routes and servicing norms. Also, dispatch plans, auto replenishment systems have been aligned.
We are about 82% done on the national rollout. By first week of February, we will be 100% done.

chain_of_thought: The earnings call highlighted Tata Consumer Products' agility in response to climate change, commitment to responsible business ratings, strong innovation pipeline, and strategic growth initiatives. However, there was limited specific discussion on environmental metrics and social impact beyond product innovation and market expansion.
weaknesses: Lack of detailed social impact initiatives
